## Emerald 1.4.1

A fixes-and-polish release, plus **Templates**.

### New
- **Templates.** Point **Settings → Templates folder** at a folder in your
  vault, then **Insert Template…** (`Ctrl+T`) opens a quick picker of every note
  under it (sub-folders included) and drops the chosen one in at the caret.
  Templates can carry `{{date}}`, `{{time}}` and `{{title}}` placeholders, each
  filled in on insert; `{{date}}`/`{{time}}` take an optional Moment.js-style
  format after a colon (e.g. `{{date:YYYY/MM/DD}}`, `{{time:HH:mm:ss}}`).
- **Overlapping emphasis.** Bold / italic / strikethrough / highlight now nest
  and stack, so `==dog ~~cat *horse **elephant***~~==` layers all four styles
  incrementally instead of one winning.
- **Smarter pipe tables.** Enter on a header row auto-adds the `---` separator
  and a first data row (caret lands in the first cell); Tab walks/grows the grid
  and re-aligns the columns on every press; Enter on the last row leaves the
  table.

### Fixes
- **List continuation** with text after the caret — pressing Enter mid-item now
  splits the line, carrying the text after the caret onto a new marked item.
- **Italic after bold** on the same line now renders (e.g. `**bold** *italic*`).
- **Deleted notes** no longer linger in the back/forward history, and navigating
  to a stale entry can no longer re-create the file on save.
- A completed task's **strikethrough** now stacks with bold / italic / highlight
  on a word inside it instead of being overwritten.
- The left pane's header is now green.

### Downloads
| Platform | File | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Linux (x86-64) | `Emerald-x86_64.AppImage` | `chmod +x` and run |
| Linux (ARM64)  | `Emerald-aarch64.AppImage` | `chmod +x` and run · glibc ≥ 2.39 |
| macOS (universal) | `Emerald-macOS.dmg` | drag to Applications; first launch **right-click → Open → Open**. If still blocked: `xattr -cr /Applications/emerald.app` |
| Windows | `Emerald-win64.zip` | extract and run `emerald.exe` |

Every download bundles its own Qt runtime — nothing else to install. Built
automatically by the `Release` GitHub Actions workflow.
